title: cistaceae
intro: The Cistaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as rock roses or rockroses. This family consists of approximately 140 species, mostly shrubs and small trees, found in the Mediterranean region and other parts of the world with similar climates. Cistaceae plants are typically characterized by their showy flowers, which are often pink, white, or purple in color, and their ability to thrive in well-drained soils and full sun. The family Cistaceae is relatively small compared to other plant families, but its members are highly valued for their beauty and ability to add color to dry, rocky areas.
